LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-18-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CLOUD4IA LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CLOUD4IA LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SHOW THAT HE WILL BE PREJUDICED BY THE FILING OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OR THAT DEFENDANT DELAYED UNDULY OR IS ACTING IN BAD FAITH. (CODE CIV. PROC. SEC. 426.50.) COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. Off Calendar Mar-19-2021 Off Calendar

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside July 28, 2020 Order Pursuant To Ccp Section 473(B)
Off Calendar Feb-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CLOUD4IA LLC's Motion To Set Aside July 28, 2020 Order Pursuant To CCP Section 473(B).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Cloud4ia LLC's "motion to set aside July 28, 2020 order pursuant to CCP section 473(b)" is denied. The July 28 order overruled defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint. Defendant then answered the complaint on August 21, 2020. Defendant cites no authority for the notion that a demurrer may be considered (or reconsidered) after an answer is filed. Defendant's arguments about the complaint can be litigated as the case proceeds.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside July 28, 2020 Order Pursuant To Ccp Section 473(B)
Off Calendar Jan-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CLOUD4IA, LLC's Motion To Set Aside July 28, 2020 Order Pursuant To CCP Section 473(B).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is off calendar. Defendant did not file and serve the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1005.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CLOUD4IA LLC's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Cloud4ia LLC's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. Plaintiff does not show that he will be prejudiced by the filing of the cross-complaint or that Defendant delayed unduly or is acting in bad faith. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 426.50.)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Set For Trial On Apr-05-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Christine Robles (crobles@slatelawgroup.com) for defendant/moving party.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court noted counsel did not comply with SFLR regarding tentative ruling rule. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CLOUD4IA LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT:

Defendant Cloud4ia LLC's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is overruled in its entirety. Both parties agree they enetered into a valid agreement in August 2019. (See Complaint, Ex. A.) Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a cause of action for breach of contract. Plaintiff alleges defendant breached the written agreement. (See Compl. 29 [alleging defendants breached the Agreement by failing to pay Plaintiff monthly draw in the amount of $7,500 for the month of October 2019].) (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872 ["To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged."]; see also Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38 ["If the complaint states a cause of action under any theory, regardless of the title under which the factual basis for relief is stated, that aspect of the complaint is good against a demurrer."].) Plaintiff further alleges that at the expiration of the 90 days, the parties verbally agreed for plaintiff to continue to provide services under the agreement. (Id. 15.) Plaintiff also alleges that on December 17, 2019, defendant informed plaintiff that it "will no longer be using your services," which supports plaintiff's allegation that both parties agreed to extend the contract past the 90-day period. (Id. 18.) Plaintiff's allegations are also sufficient to plead breach of an oral agreement. (See id. 30-31, 34-35 [describing defendant's alleged failure to pay plaintiff from November through December 2019].)

The second cause of action, for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, rises and falls with the existence of a contract between the two parties. (See Pacific States Enterprises, Inc. v. City of Coachella (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 1414, 1425.) As discussed above, the complaint alleges sufficient facts to plead a breach of contract claim.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Christine Robles (crobles@slatelawgroup.com) for defendant/moving party.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court noted counsel did not comply with SFLR regarding tentative ruling rule. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CLOUD4IA LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT:

Defendant Cloud4ia's motion to strike plaintiff's complaint is denied in its entirety. All of the paragraphs of the complaint that defendant seeks to strike contain allegations that are necessary to plaintiff's complaint, which sufficiently and succinctly lay out the elements of a breach of contract claim. Defendant does not show that any of those allegations is "irrelevant, false, or improper" within the meaning of Code Civ. Proc. 436. To the extent that defendant disagrees with the factual accuracy of those allegations, the proper vehicle for challenging them is a motion for summary judgment, not a motion to strike.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
